slack: Shipped the first in-repo tool registry slice for GitHub and Linear, including schemas for open PR counts, stale PRs, backlog counts, and issue lists.
Implemented registry-backed tool contracts so tool steps can omit inline output schemas when a registry entry exists.
Built the first raw-vs-Runeflow evaluation harness and started comparing token usage and reliability.
gdrive: Drafted the hybrid-skills roadmap covering host ownership, runtime semantics, hooks, and tool registry priorities.
Outlined an evaluation plan for raw skills versus Runeflow across GitHub, Linear, and mixed workflows.
Documented the architecture boundary: host owns lifecycle, Runeflow owns workflow, LLM handles bounded generation.
email: Identified Cerebras token-per-minute quota limits as a practical issue for benchmark fairness when running raw and Runeflow back to back.
Discussed using smaller models or delayed sequential runs for fairer hosted-model comparisons.
Collected feedback that tool contracts should come from a registry instead of being repeated in every workflow file.
calendar: Reviewed the prototype direction for hybrid text-plus-execution skills and agreed to prioritize tool registry work ahead of deeper composition features.
Aligned on adding lifecycle hooks so hosts can intervene around steps without taking over workflow orchestration.
